author: @mark.guitar
text: This is basically what I said at the beginning of the year for any new students in my class. It saved everyone a lot of time and was a central part of the class dynamic. "Engage brain, engage mouth (check you're right), engage with the workpiece, repeat."

author: @scfcrob
text:
The negatives are far outweighed by the positives but we all tend to focus on the naysayers than the majority. 
Do even more on the positive responses and don’t let the naysayers get under your skin.
Keep up the inspirational work Rob, you’ve certainly got me creating

author: @dr.froghopper6711
text: Engage my brain? That’s why I watch you, mate. I’m disabled and my body doesn’t engage well, but my brain needs regular feeding!

author: @Akolgo_islam
text:
Im a roofer & im often asked in advance to propose a solution, even if the problem hasn’t been diagnosed. Then to price that repair 😂 some people can’t get their head round the uncertainty & how that affects the overall cost. 
I say it’s a bit like asking a farmer exactly how many litres of water he’ll use next summer, yeah he can give you an answer but the only garauntee is that that answer will be almost certainly WRONG! (And someone has to pay for the error!)

replies.author: @0101-f4d
replies.text: I bet the "someone" they have in mind to cover the gap is you. Hanlon's Razor is just a cliché adage as far as I am concerned, when money is involved one should always presume the other party knows what they are doing.

replies.author: @Akolgo_islam
replies.text: @0101-f4d Yeah that someone is me! Slowly I’m getting wise enough to charge for worst case scenario if that’s what they are afraid of

author: @Mosfet510
text: I enjoy the videos & Happy New Year!

author: @ibme8359
text: OK, IB guilty, on occasion (not watching the entire video before commenting)!!! I did watch this one to the end before commenting! We should be more patient, but we may get excited as we are wanting to help!!!
